title: Match mobile UI colors to desktop and improve cross-device responsiveness

requirements.text: Ensure all color tokens, backgrounds, text colors, accent colors, and component styles are consistently applied across all screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop) — no visual discrepancies between breakpoints. All pages (Dashboard, Breathe, Sounds, Journal, Analytics, Profile, Settings, Sleep, VibrationControl, TipsAndReminders) must render with identical color themes regardless of viewport width.

quotes: Make it same as in desktop too like colors and responsive

acceptanceCriteria: Color tokens defined in frontend/src/index.css and frontend/index.css are applied uniformly at all breakpoints
Dark and light mode colors match between mobile and desktop viewports on all pages
No page has a different background, card, or text color on mobile vs desktop
Gradient, accent, and chart colors are consistent across all screen sizes

requirements.text: Make the app fully responsive across all screen sizes (mobile 320px+, tablet 768px+, desktop 1024px+). All layouts, grids, cards, charts, and navigation elements must adapt gracefully. Pages with fixed widths or overflowing content must be corrected. The bottom navigation should remain on mobile/tablet, and a sidebar or top navigation may be introduced for desktop if appropriate.

quotes: Make it same as in desktop too like colors and responsive

acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scrollbar or overflow on any page at any standard viewport width
Grid layouts (e.g., sound tiles, stats cards) reflow correctly from 1-column mobile to 2–3 column desktop
StressChart, StressGauge, and all Recharts visualizations resize fluidly without clipping
Header and BottomNav are correctly positioned and sized on mobile, tablet, and desktop
Typography scales appropriately across breakpoints using Tailwind responsive classes
All forms, sliders, and modals (e.g., ProfileSetupModal) are usable and correctly sized on all screen widths
EmergencyCalmOverlay fills the screen correctly on all device sizes

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Do not change the backend schema or Motoko logic
Preserve all existing design tokens and OKLCH color palette — only fix inconsistent application of them
nonGoals: Changing the visual design language or color palette itself
